husband.en: a married man, in relation to his spouse

ex.en: Her husband works at a hospital.

i.en: used by a speaker to refer to himself or herself

ex.en: I live in Seoul.

ice.en: water that has frozen and become solid

ex.en: Can I have some ice in my drink?
ex.ko: 음료에 얼음 좀 넣어줄 수 있어요?

ice cream.en: a sweet frozen food made from cream, milk, and sugar

ex.en: The kids love chocolate ice cream.

idea.en: a thought or plan about what to do

ex.en: She had a great idea for the project.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그 프로젝트에 대한 좋은 아이디어가 있었다.

ideal.en: the best possible for a particular situation or purpose

ex.en: This is the ideal place for a picnic.

ideal.en: a perfect standard or example that people hope to achieve

ex.en: He never gave up on his ideals.
ex.ko: 그는 자신의 이상을 결코 포기하지 않았다.

if.en: used to talk about something that might happen or be true

ex.en: If it rains, we will stay home.
ex.ko: 만약 비가 오면, 우리는 집에 있을 것이다.

imagine.en: form a picture or idea of something in your mind

ex.en: Imagine a world without smartphones.

imagine.en: suppose or guess that something is true

ex.en: I imagine she's already home by now.
ex.ko: 지금쯤 그녀는 이미 집에 도착했을 것 같다.

important.en: having great value, meaning, or effect

ex.en: Family is the most important thing to her.

in.en: inside a place, area, or period of time

ex.en: The keys are in my bag.
The website has useful information about the city.
ex.ko: 그 웹사이트에는 그 도시에 대한 유용한 정보가 있다.

inside.en: in or into the inner part of a place

ex.en: Let's go inside, it's cold out here.

inside.en: the inner part or area of something

ex.en: The inside of the box was empty.

interested.en: wanting to know more about or take part in something

ex.en: She's interested in learning French.
ex.ko: 그녀는 프랑스어 배우는 것에 관심이 있다.

interesting.en: making you want to know or learn more; holding your attention

ex.en: That's a really interesting story.

internet.en: the worldwide system of connected computer networks

ex.en: I looked up the recipe on the Internet.

interview.en: ask someone a series of questions in a formal meeting

ex.en: The reporter interviewed the mayor.

interview.en: a formal meeting where someone is asked questions

ex.en: She has a job interview tomorrow.

interviewer.en: a person who asks the questions in an interview

ex.en: The interviewer asked her about her experience.

into.en: to the inside of a place

ex.en: She walked into the room.

introduce.en: tell someone another person's name when they meet for the first time

ex.en: Let me introduce you to my sister.

introduce.en: bring something new into use for the first time

ex.en: The school introduced a new dress code.
ex.ko: 그 학교는 새로운 복장 규정을 도입했다.

is.en: used to describe or identify a singular subject

ex.en: She is a doctor.

island.en: a piece of land completely surrounded by water

ex.en: They spent their vacation on a small island.

it.en: the use of computers and telecommunications to manage information

ex.en: She works in the IT department.

item.en: a single thing on a list

ex.en: There are ten items on the agenda.
This store sells clothing items only.

its.en: belonging to a thing or animal that was mentioned before

ex.en: The dog wagged its tail.
He put on his jacket before going outside.
ex.ko: 그는 밖에 나가기 전에 재킷을 입었다.

january.en: the first month of the year

ex.en: Her birthday is in January.

jeans.en: trousers made of strong cotton cloth called denim

ex.en: He was wearing a T-shirt and jeans.

jet.en: an airplane powered by jet engines

ex.en: The jet took off from the runway.

jewelry.en: decorative items such as rings and necklaces made of precious metals or stones

ex.en: She keeps her jewelry in a small box.

job.en: the work you do regularly to earn money

ex.en: She got a new job at a bank.

job.en: a specific task or piece of work that needs to be done

ex.en: Cleaning the garage is your job this weekend.
ex.ko: 이번 주말에는 차고 청소가 네가 할 일이다.

join.en: become a member of a group or organization

ex.en: He joined a soccer club last year.

join.en: come together with other people to do something

ex.en: Why don't you join us for dinner?

join.en: connect or fasten two things together

ex.en: The bridge joins the two islands.

judge.en: an official who decides cases in a court of law

ex.en: The judge sentenced him to two years in prison.

judge.en: a person who decides who wins a competition

ex.en: The judges gave her a perfect score.

judge.en: form an opinion about something after thinking carefully

ex.en: Don't judge people by their appearance.

juice.en: the liquid from fruit or vegetables that you drink

ex.en: She drinks orange juice every morning.
ex.ko: 그녀는 매일 아침 오렌지 주스를 마신다.

july.en: the month following June and preceding August

ex.en: We usually go on vacation in July.

jump.en: a sudden large increase or change

ex.en: There was a big jump in sales this year.

jump.en: push yourself off the ground using your legs

ex.en: The children jumped over the puddle.

june.en: the month following May and preceding July

ex.en: School usually ends in June.

just.cue: 그 정도밖에 안 됨을 나타낼 때

ex.en: It's just a small mistake.
I just finished my homework.
That's just what I wanted.

keep.en: cause something to stay in a particular state or condition

ex.en: Please keep the room clean.
She kept talking for an hour.
ex.ko: 그녀는 한 시간 동안 계속 이야기했다.

keep.en: have or store something in a particular place

ex.en: He keeps his passport in a drawer.

key.en: a small metal object used to lock or unlock something

ex.en: I forgot my house key at home.
ex.ko: 나는 집 열쇠를 집에 두고 왔다.

key.en: the most important thing needed for success or understanding

ex.en: Practice is the key factor in learning a language.
ex.ko: 연습이 언어 학습에서 가장 핵심적인 요소이다.

ex.en: He kicked the ball into the goal.

kick.en: the act of hitting something with your foot

ex.en: The horse gave a strong kick.

kid.en: say something that is not true as a joke

ex.en: I'm just kidding, don't worry.
ex.ko: 나 그냥 농담하는 거야, 걱정하지 마.

ex.en: The kids are playing in the yard.

kill.en: cause a person, animal, or plant to die

ex.en: The frost killed all the flowers in the garden.

kill.en: cause someone great pain or difficulty (informal)

ex.en: My feet are killing me after the long walk.

king.en: a male ruler of a country

ex.en: The king lived in a huge castle.

kiss.en: touch someone with your lips to show love or greeting

ex.en: He kissed her on the cheek.
She gave her son a kiss goodnight.
ex.ko: 그녀는 아들에게 잘 자라는 입맞춤을 해주었다.

kitchen.en: a room where food is prepared and cooked

ex.en: She's cooking dinner in the kitchen.

kite.en: a light toy that flies in the wind at the end of a string

ex.en: The boy flew a kite in the park.

knee.en: the joint in the middle of the leg where it bends

ex.en: She fell and hurt her knee.

knife.en: a tool with a sharp blade used for cutting

ex.en: Be careful with that knife.

know.en: have information or knowledge about something

ex.en: I don't know his phone number.

know.en: be familiar or acquainted with a person

ex.en: Do you know that woman over there?
She knows how to swim.

lady.en: a polite word for a woman

ex.en: There's a lady waiting at the door.
ex.ko: 문 앞에 한 여성이 기다리고 있다.

language.en: a system of words used by people to communicate

ex.en: She speaks three languages.

large.en: bigger than average in size or amount

ex.en: They live in a large house.

last name.en: the family name that comes after a person's first name

ex.en: Please write your first name and last name.

late.en: happening or arriving after the expected time

ex.en: I was late for the meeting.

late.en: after the usual or expected time

ex.en: She usually goes to bed late.

later.en: at a time after the present or after the time mentioned

ex.en: I'll call you later.

later.en: coming after in time or order

ex.en: We'll discuss this in a later chapter.
ex.ko: 이 내용은 나중 장에서 논의할 것이다.

laugh.en: make sounds and movements that show you find something funny

ex.en: Everyone laughed at his joke.

laugh.en: the sound or act of laughing

ex.en: I heard a laugh coming from the next room.

lazy.en: not willing to work or do things

ex.en: He's too lazy to clean his room.
ex.ko: 그는 너무 게을러서 방을 청소하지 않는다.

leader.en: a person who leads or guides a group

ex.en: She is a natural leader.

leaf.en: the flat green part of a plant that grows from a stem or branch

ex.en: The leaves turn yellow in autumn.

learn.en: gain knowledge or a skill through study or practice

ex.en: She is learning how to play the piano.
ex.ko: 그녀는 피아노 치는 법을 배우고 있다.

learn.en: get to know a fact, often by chance

ex.en: We learned that the flight was delayed.
ex.ko: 우리는 비행기가 지연되었다는 것을 알게 되었다.

left.en: on or toward the side opposite the right

ex.en: Turn left at the corner.
The bank is on your left.

leg.cue: 걷고 서는 데 쓰는 신체 부위
leg.en: one of the two long parts of the body used for standing and walking

ex.en: He broke his leg while skiing.

lesson.en: a period of time in which something is taught

ex.en: We have a math lesson at nine.

lesson.en: something you learn from an experience, especially a mistake

ex.en: This mistake taught him an important lesson.
ex.ko: 이 실수는 그에게 중요한 교훈을 주었다.

let.en: allow someone or something to do something

ex.en: My parents let me stay out late.
ex.ko: 부모님은 내가 늦게까지 밖에 있는 것을 허락하셨다.

letter.en: a written message that you send to someone

ex.en: She wrote a letter to her grandmother.

letter.en: one of the symbols used to write a language, such as A or B

ex.en: The word 'cat' has three letters.

library.en: a place where books are kept for people to read or borrow

ex.en: I borrowed this book from the library.

life.en: the period between birth and death, or the experience of living

ex.en: She had a happy life.

life.en: the state of being alive, as opposed to being dead

ex.en: The doctors saved his life.
I like listening to music.

like.en: similar to or in the same way as something else

ex.en: She sings like a professional.

lily.en: a plant with large, showy flowers, often white

ex.en: She placed lilies on the table.

line.en: stand or arrange in a row, one behind another

ex.en: People lined up to buy tickets.

line.en: a long, thin mark on a surface

ex.en: Draw a straight line across the page.

line.en: a row of people or things

ex.en: There was a long line at the checkout.

lion.cue: 갈기가 있는 아프리카의 큰 고양잇과 동물
lion.en: a large wild cat with a mane, found in Africa

ex.en: The lion roared loudly.

list.en: write down a series of items one after another

ex.en: She listed all the things she needed to buy.
ex.ko: 그녀는 사야 할 것들을 목록으로 만들었다.

list.en: a set of names or items written down one after another

ex.en: Check the list before you leave.

listen.en: pay attention in order to hear something

ex.en: Listen to the teacher carefully.

little.en: not much; a small amount of something

ex.en: We have little time left.
They live in a little house by the lake.

live.en: have your home in a particular place

ex.en: They live in a small apartment.

live.en: broadcast or performed at the actual time it happens, not recorded

ex.en: The concert was broadcast live on TV.

living.en: the money you need to pay for the things you need to live

ex.en: She earns her living as a writer.
He is the last living member of the family.

living room.en: a room in a house where people sit and relax

ex.en: We watched TV together in the living room.

lonely.en: unhappy because you are alone or have no friends nearby

ex.en: He felt lonely after moving to a new city.
ex.ko: 그는 새로운 도시로 이사한 후 외로움을 느꼈다.

long.en: measuring a great distance from end to end

ex.en: She has long hair.

long.en: for a large amount of time

ex.en: We waited long for the bus.

look.en: turn your eyes toward something in order to see it

ex.en: Look at the beautiful sunset.

look.en: give a certain impression when seen

ex.en: You look tired today.
She helped me a lot with my project.

lot.en: a piece of land with fixed borders, used for a particular purpose

ex.en: They bought a parking lot downtown.
ex.ko: 그들은 시내에 있는 주차장 부지를 샀다.

love.en: a strong feeling of deep affection for someone

ex.en: Their love grew stronger over the years.
ex.ko: 그들의 사랑은 세월이 지나면서 더 깊어졌다.

ex.en: He loves his family very much.
I love this song.

lovely.en: beautiful or attractive in a way that pleases you

ex.en: What a lovely garden!
We had a lovely time at the beach.

luck.en: good things that happen to you by chance

ex.en: Good luck on your exam!

lucky.en: having good things happen to you by chance

ex.en: I was lucky to find a parking spot.
ex.ko: 나는 운 좋게 주차 자리를 찾았다.

lunch.en: the meal you eat in the middle of the day

ex.en: Let's have lunch together.

machine.en: a device with moving parts that does a particular job using power

ex.en: This machine washes clothes automatically.

magazine.en: a publication with pictures and articles that comes out regularly

ex.en: She reads a fashion magazine every month.

mail.en: send a letter or package through the postal service

ex.en: I mailed the package yesterday.
ex.ko: 나는 어제 그 소포를 우편으로 보냈다.

mail.en: letters and packages sent through the postal service

ex.en: Did the mail arrive yet?

man.en: take charge of or operate something, such as a job or position

ex.en: Two workers man the front desk at all times.
ex.ko: 직원 두 명이 항상 안내 데스크를 담당한다.

ex.en: A man was walking his dog in the park.
ex.ko: 한 남자가 공원에서 개를 산책시키고 있었다.

many.en: a large number of (used with countable nouns)

ex.en: There are many books on the shelf.

map.en: a drawing that shows the features of an area, such as roads or countries

ex.en: Can you show me on the map where we are?
ex.ko: 우리가 어디 있는지 지도에서 보여줄 수 있어?

march.en: the month following February and preceding April

ex.en: Spring begins in March.

march.en: walk with regular steps, often as a group

ex.en: The soldiers marched through the town.

march.en: an organized walk by a group of people, often in protest or celebration

ex.en: Thousands joined the march for climate action.
ex.ko: 수천 명이 기후 행동을 위한 행진에 참여했다.

math.en: the study of numbers, shapes, and quantities

ex.en: He is good at math.
Your opinion matters to us.

matter.en: a subject or situation that you need to deal with

ex.en: We need to discuss this matter later.
ex.ko: 우리는 이 문제를 나중에 논의해야 한다.

may.en: the month following April and preceding June

ex.en: They got married in May.

maybe.en: used to say that something is possible but not certain

ex.en: Maybe it will rain tomorrow.

me.en: used to refer to the speaker as the object of a verb or preposition

ex.en: Can you help me?

meal.en: the food eaten at one particular time, such as breakfast or dinner

ex.en: We had a big meal at the restaurant.

meat.en: the flesh of an animal eaten as food

ex.en: He doesn't eat meat.

medicine.en: a substance used to treat or prevent illness

ex.en: Take this medicine after meals.

medicine.en: the science of treating and preventing illness

ex.en: She studies medicine at university.

meet.en: come together with someone, by chance or arrangement

ex.en: Let's meet at the cafe at noon.

meet.en: satisfy a need, requirement, or condition

ex.en: The product meets all safety standards.
ex.ko: 그 제품은 모든 안전 기준을 충족한다.

meeting.en: a gathering of people to discuss or decide something

ex.en: We have a meeting at three o'clock.
She has a great memory for names.

memory.en: something you remember from the past

ex.en: I have happy memories of my childhood.
ex.ko: 나는 어린 시절의 행복한 추억이 있다.

ex.en: Merry Christmas to you and your family!
ex.ko: 당신과 가족 모두 즐거운 크리스마스 보내세요!

message.en: a piece of information sent from one person to another

ex.en: She sent me a text message.

middle.cue: 양 끝에서 같은 거리에 있는 지점
middle.en: the point or part that is equally distant from the edges or ends

ex.en: He stood in the middle of the room.

middle.en: being neither at the beginning nor at the end

ex.en: She sat in the middle seat.

milk.en: a white liquid produced by cows and other animals, used as food or drink

ex.en: She drinks a glass of milk every morning.
ex.ko: 그녀는 매일 아침 우유 한 잔을 마신다.

mind.en: the part of a person that thinks, feels, and remembers

ex.en: Keep an open mind.

mind.en: feel annoyed or bothered by something

ex.en: Do you mind if I open the window?

mine.en: a place where minerals such as coal or gold are dug from the ground

ex.en: His grandfather worked in a coal mine.

minute.en: a unit of time equal to 60 seconds

ex.en: Wait a minute, please.

mobile.cue: 쉽게 움직이거나 들고 다닐 수 있는
mobile.en: able to move or be moved easily from place to place

ex.en: She works from a mobile office.

mobile phone.en: a telephone that you can carry with you and use anywhere

ex.en: I left my mobile phone at home.
Mom, can you help me with my homework?
ex.ko: 엄마, 숙제 좀 도와주실 수 있어요?

moment.en: a very short period of time

ex.en: Wait a moment, I'll be right there.

mommy.en: a child's affectionate word for mother

ex.en: Mommy, look what I made!

monday.en: the day after Sunday and before Tuesday

ex.en: I have a meeting on Monday.

money.en: coins and paper notes used to buy things

ex.en: He doesn't have enough money for a new bike.
ex.ko: 그는 새 자전거를 살 돈이 충분하지 않다.

monkey.en: an animal with a long tail that climbs trees, related to apes

ex.en: The monkey swung from branch to branch.

month.en: one of the twelve parts that a year is divided into

ex.en: We're going on vacation next month.
ex.ko: 우리는 다음 달에 휴가를 갈 것이다.

moon.en: the large object that moves around the Earth and shines at night

ex.en: The moon was full and bright last night.

more.en: a greater amount or degree of something

ex.en: Can I have more coffee, please?
ex.ko: 커피 좀 더 마실 수 있을까요?

morning.en: the early part of the day, from sunrise until noon

ex.en: I go jogging every morning.

most.en: used to form the superlative, meaning to the greatest degree

ex.en: This is the most beautiful place I've ever seen.
ex.ko: 여기는 내가 본 곳 중 가장 아름다운 곳이다.

most.en: the majority of people or things

ex.en: Most students passed the exam.

mother.en: a woman who has a child

ex.en: Her mother is a teacher.

mountain.en: a very high piece of land that rises above the surrounding area

ex.en: They climbed the mountain in five hours.
ex.ko: 그들은 다섯 시간 만에 그 산을 올랐다.

mouse.en: a small animal with a long tail

ex.en: A mouse ran across the kitchen floor.
ex.ko: 쥐 한 마리가 부엌 바닥을 가로질러 뛰어갔다.

mouse.en: a small device used to control the cursor on a computer screen

ex.en: Click the mouse to open the file.

mouth.en: the opening in your face used for eating and speaking

ex.en: Open your mouth and say ah.
Please move your car, it's blocking the gate.
ex.ko: 차 좀 옮겨 주세요, 문을 막고 있어요.

move.en: go to live in a different house or place

ex.en: They moved to a new apartment last month.

movie.en: a story shown on a screen with moving pictures and sound

ex.en: We watched a movie last night.

much.en: a large amount of something (used with uncountable nouns)

ex.en: I don't have much time today.

mum.en: a British informal word for mother

ex.en: I'll ask my mum if I can come.
ex.ko: 내가 갈 수 있는지 엄마한테 물어볼게.

music.en: sounds arranged in a pleasing way, made with instruments or voices

ex.en: She listens to music while studying.

musician.en: a person who plays or creates music, especially as a job

ex.en: He wants to become a professional musician.

must.cue: 반드시 해야 할 의무를 나타낼 때
must.en: used to say that something is necessary or very important to do

ex.en: You must wear a seatbelt in the car.
ex.ko: 차 안에서는 반드시 안전벨트를 매야 한다.

my.cue: 말하는 사람이 가진 것을 나타낼 때

ex.en: This is my bag.

name.en: the word or words that a person or thing is called by

ex.en: What's your name?

name.en: give a name to a person or thing

ex.en: They named their daughter Emily.

nationality.en: the country that a person is legally a citizen of

ex.en: What is your nationality?
The station is near my house.
Come near so I can see you.
ex.ko: 내가 볼 수 있게 가까이 와.

neck.en: the part of the body that connects the head to the shoulders

ex.en: She wore a scarf around her neck.

need.cue: 꼭 있어야 하는 상태를 나타낼 때
need.en: require something because it is necessary

ex.en: I need some water.
There is a growing need for clean energy.
ex.ko: 청정 에너지에 대한 필요성이 커지고 있다.

neighbor.en: a person who lives near you

ex.en: Our neighbor helped us move the furniture.
ex.ko: 우리 이웃이 가구 옮기는 것을 도와줬다.

never.en: not at any time; not ever

ex.en: I have never been to Japan.
ex.ko: 나는 일본에 한 번도 가본 적이 없다.

new.cue: 만들어지거나 얻은 지 얼마 안 된
new.en: recently made, bought, or discovered; not old

ex.en: She bought a new phone.
Did you hear the news about the election?

newspaper.en: a printed publication with news, usually issued daily or weekly

ex.en: He reads the newspaper every morning.
It was nice to meet you.

night.en: the time when it is dark, between evening and morning

ex.en: We stayed up all night talking.
She has nine cousins.
He turned nineteen last week.
